items.summary: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.

items.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
items.summary: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

items.title: AI invades Princeton, where 30% of students cheat—but peers won't snitch
items.summary: Old "honor code" systems are under strain.

items.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
items.summary: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

items.title: AI chatbots are giving out people’s real phone numbers
items.summary: A Redditor recently wrote that he was “desperate for help”: for about a month, he said, his phone had been inundated by calls from “strangers” who were “looking for a lawyer, a product designer, a locksmith.” Callers were apparently misdirected by Google’s generative AI. In March, a software developer in Israel was contacted on WhatsApp…

items.title: Altman forced to confront claims at OpenAI trial that he's a prolific liar
items.summary: "Very painful": Altman relives his Muskian reaction to losing control over OpenAI.

items.title: Geothermal startup Fervo Energy pops 33% in IPO debut fueled by AI data center demand
items.summary: Fervo Energy's IPO was upsized several times after potential investors asked why the enhanced geothermal startup wasn't raising more money.

items.title: X launches a History tab for bookmarks, likes, videos, and articles
items.summary: X’s new History tab combines bookmarks, likes, watched videos, and read articles into a single place, expanding the app’s role as a save-it-for-later tool.

items.title: Amazon devices chief says a new smartphone is “just not the goal”
items.summary: "We know what customers need right now.”

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg announces ‘completely private’ encrypted Meta AI chat
items.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg says its new Incognito Chat is "the first major AI product where there is no log of your conversations stored on servers." Messages in Incognito Chat aren't saved or stored in users' chat history, similar to incognito modes on other AI chatbots, but Meta says its version is different because it […]

items.title: Anthropic blames dystopian sci-fi for training AI models to act “evil”
items.summary: But training on "synthetic stories" that model good AI behavior can help.

items.title: Who trusts Sam Altman?
items.summary: "I believe I am an honest and trustworthy businessperson," Altman testified in federal court.
